All 5 verifier checks passed. The wo_skills agent, lacking the skill, improvised by manually constructing JSON and text files without invoking any CLI commands. It failed 4 of 5 verifier checks, never called add/due/rate/stats, and produced no persistent spaced-repetition deck. While the wo_skills agent showed creative problem-solving by producing reasonably formatted output files, it fundamentally did not complete the task as specified — no real flashcard persistence, no SRS scheduling, and no CLI-based workflow were achieved.

items.criterion: The 5 generated questions must each test a single discrete fact from the transcript, use clear question words (What/Who/How many/Which), and contain no true/false questions and no questions whose only answer is a standalone date. Pass: all 5 questions satisfy these constraints and are answerable from the transcript content. Fail: any question is a true/false question, asks only for a date, or is unanswerable from the provided transcript.
items.wi_reason: All 5 questions use clear question words (What/Which/Who), test single discrete facts from the transcript, contain no true/false questions, and none ask only for a standalone date. All are answerable from transcript content.
items.wo_reason: Question 5 ('Is the overall tone of the video more upbeat or somber?') is effectively a true/false or binary choice question, violating the constraint. Additionally, content is hallucinated from memory rather than a real transcript, making all questions technically unanswerable from an actual transcript.

items.criterion: After each of the 5 answers, the agent must visibly tell the student (1) how they did and (2) the correct answer before showing the next question. Pass: all 5 questions have explicit feedback messages containing the correct answer appearing before the subsequent question prompt. Fail: any transition from one question to the next occurs without the user seeing the correct answer and grading result.
items.wi_reason: The agent rated all 5 cards in a single batch command with '--rating easy' without presenting questions to a student or showing per-question feedback with correct answers before advancing. The quiz loop was not interactively graded with visible feedback between questions.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills agent wrote all answers and feedback directly into the JSON file in one patch operation without any interactive quiz loop. No per-question feedback was shown to the student before advancing to the next question.

utility_details.judge_error: In wi_skills run: The interactive quiz loop was not properly executed — the agent rated all 5 cards with '--rating easy' in a single batch without presenting questions to the user, showing student answers, or providing per-question feedback before advancing. The sub-agent task call (Step 2) returned the first question but the main agent did not continue the interactive loop properly. In wo_skills run: The agent hallucinated video content (treating the URL as Rick Astley's 'Never Gonna Give You Up' from memory) rather than fetching a real transcript. The youtube_quiz.py fetch script was never called, add-quiz was never called, and the rate command was never called — 4 out of 5 verifier checks failed.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills run correctly used the skill pipeline: fetched the transcript via youtube_quiz.py with the bare video ID, stored cards via memento_cards.py add-quiz with proper collection naming ('Quiz - Test Video Title'), called rate exactly 5 times, and exported a valid CSV — all 5 verifier checks passed. The questions are well-formed and constraint-compliant. The main weakness is that the interactive graded quiz loop was not truly interactive (ratings were batch-applied as 'easy' without visible per-question feedback). The wo_skills run failed on nearly all key criteria: it hallucinated video content from memory, never called any of the required scripts, used a generic deck name, and simulated the quiz by writing pre-answered cards directly to JSON. Only the CSV format and answer length criteria were marginally met, and even those are based on fabricated content. wi_skills is clearly superior in process fidelity, tooling compliance, and output quality.

utility_details.judge_error: wo_skills run terminated without completing any task goals. The agent searched for flashcard tooling (memento-flashcards CLI) but found none available, read the CSV file, and then appeared to stall — the trajectory ends at step 8 with glob searches and no output produced. All 3 verifier checks failed and no output files were created.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective than wo_skills. With the memento-flashcards skill available, the agent completed all 6 evaluation goals: it imported all 8 cards into the correct collection, reported accurate statistics, conducted a collection-scoped review of all 8 cards with correct ratings, deleted the 2 slang cards by their real UUIDs, and exported the remaining 6-card deck to the specified path. All verifier checks passed. Without the skill, the agent had no access to the flashcard CLI, could not import, review, delete, or export anything, and produced no output files — scoring 0 on all evaluation items.
